title: eria soronensis
side_effects: Eria soronensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing anxiety, panic attacks, and rapid heartbeat due to its high concentration of alkaloids and phenylethanoid glycosides. Overuse of the plant can also lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as a result of its stimulant properties. In rare cases, Eria soronensis can cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and itching, especially in individuals with pre-existing sensitivities. Furthermore, using the plant in excess can disrupt sleep patterns and lead to insomnia, exacerbating underlying sleep disorders.
